draft.title: a bookshop website
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a bookshop website

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated page transitions over instant loads for bookshop navigation
facade.label: Page slides in smoothly
facade.acceptImplies: Build fluid, eased transitions between book categories and detail pages; motion signals intentionality and care
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize instant, zero-latency navigation; users want speed and directness over choreography

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality when browsing books online
facade.label: Warm, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: The bookshop site should use casual language, first-person recommendations, and a human touch in copy—like a friend suggesting books, not a catalog
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more polished, professional voice with formal product descriptions and neutral brand language

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer animated page transitions over instant loads for bookshop navigation
evidence.implication: Build fluid, eased transitions between book categories and detail pages; motion signals intentionality and care

facade.hypothesis: Users differ on whether they want to see many titles at once (dense grid) or fewer titles with more detail (sparse layout)
facade.label: Book count per page
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers dense, high-volume browsing—many book covers visible, minimal text, scan-and-click behavior
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers breathing room—fewer books shown, larger covers, more metadata visible per item, slower deliberate browsing

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left navigation paired with card-based product displays rather than top tabs with list layouts
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore navigation fixed to left, browse books in card grid—persistent context, discoverable categories
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer top navigation, horizontal tabs, or list-based layouts for simpler, flatter hierarchy

facade.hypothesis: Users expect bookshops to feel inviting and tactile, with natural materials and soft lighting that encourage browsing
facade.label: Warm cream & wood
facade.acceptImplies: Pursue earthy, natural color schemes with cream, beige, and wood tones; soft, ambient lighting; handcrafted or organic-feeling textures
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward cooler palettes (grays, blues, whites); sleeker, more minimalist aesthetic; brighter, more clinical lighting; digital-first visual language

facade.hypothesis: Warm, intimate mood with soft overhead lighting creates a refuge-like atmosphere that encourages lingering and browsing.
facade.label: Dim amber lighting
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward cozy, inviting, low-contrast environments. Likely prefers warm color palettes and soft visual hierarchy.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer brightness, clarity, and crisp contrast. Possibly drawn to cool tones or more energetic, lit-up atmospheres.

axes.label: Whether user prefers clean minimalist book displays vs rich visual book imagery and covers
Whether user wants browsing categories upfront vs search-first navigation for finding books
Whether user responds to enthusiastic book recommendations vs neutral informational tone
Whether user wants cozy intimate bookshop feeling vs modern sleek bookstore aesthetic
Whether user needs detailed book info (reviews, summaries, ratings) vs minimal essential details
Whether user prefers instant feedback and quick interactions vs slower contemplative browsing pace

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers clean minimalist book displays vs rich visual book imagery and covers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants browsing categories upfront vs search-first navigation for finding books
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to enthusiastic book recommendations vs neutral informational tone
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants cozy intimate bookshop feeling vs modern sleek bookstore aesthetic
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs detailed book info (reviews, summaries, ratings) vs minimal essential details
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant feedback and quick interactions vs slower contemplative browsing pace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users expect interactive feedback (elevation, shadow) on book hovers to signal clickability and create tactile sensation in a digital space
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — static affordance vs. dynamic micro-interaction
facade.label: Hover lifts book
facade.acceptImplies: Design book cards with lift/shadow on hover; micro-interactions signal interactivity and add tactile delight to browsing; motion rewards user exploration
facade.rejectImplies: Keep book cards flat and static on hover; minimize motion overhead; users prioritize quick scanning over interactive feedback; reserve motion for macro-level page transitions only

draft.title: Bookshop Homepage
draft.summary: A warm, inviting bookshop website with featured books, categories, and a search interface. Designed with earthy tones and serif typography to evoke a cozy reading experience.
